name: De Quervain's Tenosynovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial sheath surrounding the APL and EPB tendons at the radial styloid.
key_findings: Pain at radial styloid, positive Finkelstein's test, swelling.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical diagnosis. US can show fluid around tendons.

treatment_plan.acute: Thumb spica splint, ice, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Pain-free AROM, gentle isometric loading.
treatment_plan.chronic: Eccentric strengthening, functional returning.

name: Tibiofemoral Osteoarthritis (Variant 19)
description: Degenerative joint disease characterized by the breakdown of articular cartilage in the knee joint.
key_findings: Deep aching pain, morning stiffness < 30 minutes, crepitus, reduced range of motion, palpable osteophytes.
diagnostic_tips: X-ray showing joint space narrowing, osteophytes, subchondral sclerosis.
treatment_plan: Weight management, lower extremity strengthening (especially quadriceps), low-impact cardio, NSAIDs, intra-articular injections. Total knee arthroplasty in severe cases.
differential_diagnosis: Rheumatoid Arthritis, Meniscal Tear, Patellofemoral Pain Syndrome

name: Medial Meniscus Tear (Variant 19)
description: A tear in the medial C-shaped cartilage of the knee, often accompanying ACL tears (part of the unhappy triad).
key_findings: Joint line tenderness, clicking/catching, delayed swelling (effusion), locking of the knee.
diagnostic_tips: MRI confirms diagnosis. Clinical presentation and mechanism are strong indicators.

treatment_plan.acute: RICE, NSAIDs, cautious ROM.
treatment_plan.subacute: Strengthen quads and hamstrings, non-impact aerobic activity (cycling).
treatment_plan.chronic: Plyometrics and agility if repairing conservatively. Surgical meniscectomy or repair if mechanical locking persists.

name: Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) Tear (Variant 19)
description: A sprain or tear of the ACL, a major ligament in your knee. Most commonly occurs during athletic motions.
causes: Pivoting or twisting on a planted foot
key_findings: Audible 'pop', rapid swelling, giving way episodes, deep knee pain.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is the modality of choice for confirming soft tissue extension and concomitant injuries.

treatment_plan.acute: RICE protocol, crutches, hinged knee brace. Control swelling.
treatment_plan.subacute: ROM restoration (extension is critical), quad activation (SLR).
treatment_plan.chronic: Proprioceptive training, closed-chain strengthening (squats, leg press). Surigcal reconstruction often required for athletes.

name: Cellulitis (Variant 19)
description: A potentially serious bacterial skin infection causing redness, swelling, and pain in the infected area of the skin.
causes: Bacterial entry through cracks, cuts, or bug bites (usually Streptococcus or Staphylococcus)
key_findings: Red area of skin that tends to expand, swelling, tenderness, pain, warmth, fever, red spots, blisters.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical evaluation. Blood tests if spread to bloodstream is suspected.
treatment_plan: Oral or intravenous antibiotics. Elevation of the affected area. Rest. Red flag for immediate medical referral.

name: Athlete's Foot (Tinea Pedis) (Variant 19)
description: A fungal infection that usually begins between the toes, commonly occurring in people whose feet have become very sweaty while confined within tight-fitting shoes.
key_findings: Scaly, peeling, or cracked skin between the toes. Itching, especially right after taking off shoes and socks.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical diagnosis. KOH prep can confirm fungal hyphae.
treatment_plan: Topical antifungal medications (e.g., clotrimazole, terbinafine). Keep feet dry and clean. Wear breathable shoes and change socks frequently.

name: Tibiofemoral Osteoarthritis (Variant 18)
description: Degenerative joint disease characterized by the breakdown of articular cartilage in the knee joint.

name: Medial Meniscus Tear (Variant 9)
description: A tear in the medial C-shaped cartilage of the knee, often accompanying ACL tears (part of the unhappy triad).

name: Reverse Bankart Tear
description: A tear of the posterior-inferior labrum, associated with posterior shoulder dislocation.
causes: Seizure or electrical shock causing violent internal rotation
key_findings: Pain and apprehension when the arm is loaded in posterior direction (flexion, internal rotation).
diagnostic_tips: Look for reverse Hill-Sachs lesion (McLaughlin lesion) on anterior humeral head.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling immobilization, caution with horizontal adduction and internal rotation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Rotator cuff strengthening emphasizing ER and scapular retractors.
treatment_plan.chronic: Rhythmic stabilization, perturbation training.

name: Posterior Labral Tear
description: Injury to the posterior structural labrum, frequently leading to posterior shoulder instability.
causes: Direct trauma (MVA, fall on forward flexed arm)
key_findings: Posterior shoulder pain, mechanical symptoms, posterior instability.
diagnostic_tips: Can be difficult to detect clinically; often misdiagnosed as posterior cuff tendinitis.

treatment_plan.acute: Immobilization, avoid end-range internal rotation and cross-body adduction.
treatment_plan.subacute: Posterior deltoid and infraspinatus/teres minor strengthening.
treatment_plan.chronic: Return to pressing activities with restricted depth.

name: Bankart Tear (Anterior-Inferior Labrum)
description: An injury to the anterior-inferior aspect of the glenoid labrum. Often occurs as a result of anterior shoulder dislocation.
key_findings: Apprehension with extreme external rotation and abduction. Sensation of shoulder instability.
diagnostic_tips: X-ray may show bony Bankart. MRI Arthrography is best for soft tissue evaluation.

treatment_plan.acute: Immobilization in internal rotation (or external depending on protocol), avoid combined Abduction + ER.
treatment_plan.subacute: Closed kinetic chain exercises, subscapularis strengthening.
treatment_plan.chronic: Dynamic stabilization, plyometrics.

name: SLAP Tear (Superior Labrum Anterior to Posterior)
description: An injury to the superior part of the labrum from anterior to posterior, often affecting the biceps anchor.
key_findings: Deep shoulder pain, 'clicking' or 'catching' sensation, pain with overhead movement or throwing.
diagnostic_tips: MRI Arthrography is gold standard. Often presents with concomitant rotator cuff or biceps pathology.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling for comfort, cryotherapy, NSAIDs, gentle PROM.
treatment_plan.subacute: Rotator cuff and scapular stabilizer strengthening, stretching of posterior capsule.
treatment_plan.chronic: Progress to overhead and sport-specific activities. Surgical repair if conservative fails.

differential_diagnosis: Biceps tendinopathy, Rotator cuff tear, AC joint sprain

name: Cervical Radiculopathy
description: Compression or irritation of a cervical nerve root, commonly causing pain, numbness, or weakness in the upper extremity. Often due to disc herniation or spondylosis.
key_findings: Unilateral arm pain, numbness in a dermatomal pattern, weakness in myotome, decreased reflexes.
diagnostic_tips: Spurling's test positive, relief with cervical distraction, MRI to confirm root compression.

treatment_plan.acute: Pain management, cervical collar (briefly), gentle ROM, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Cervical traction, deep neck flexor strengthening, nerve gliding exercises.
treatment_plan.chronic: Postural correction, progressive resistive exercises, ergonomics.

name: Rotator Cuff Tear
description: A tear in one or more of the tendons of the rotator cuff muscles, most commonly the supraspinatus. Can be partial or full thickness.
key_findings: Pain over lateral deltoid, weakness with abduction and external rotation, night pain.
diagnostic_tips: Drop arm sign positive, painful arc, weakness on empty can test. MRI is gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling for comfort, cryotherapy, pendulum exercises, avoid active abduction.
treatment_plan.subacute: AAROM, isometrics for intact muscles, scapular stabilization.
treatment_plan.chronic: Isotonic strengthening (open and closed chain), plyometrics if returning to sports.

name: Lumbar Spondylolisthesis
description: Forward translation of one vertebral body over the adjacent vertebra below, most commonly at L4-L5 or L5-S1. Often causes mechanical low back pain or neurogenic claudication.
key_findings: Step-off deformity on palpation, pain exacerbated by extension, relief with flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Lateral lumbar X-ray (flexion/extension views to assess instability).

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid extension, NSAIDs, core bracing if severe.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbar stabilization exercises (bias flexion), hamstring stretching.
treatment_plan.chronic: Core endurance training, functional activity modification.

name: Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) Tear
description: Rupture of the ACL, a key stabilizing ligament of the knee, typically from a non-contact pivoting injury.
key_findings: Audible 'pop', immediate swelling (hemarthrosis), feeling of instability or 'giving way'.
diagnostic_tips: Lachman test is highly sensitive for acute tears. MRI for confirmation and meniscal involvement.

treatment_plan.acute: RICE, crutches, ROM restoration (especially extension), quad activation (NMES).
treatment_plan.subacute: Closed kinetic chain exercises, proprioceptive training, normalizing gait.
treatment_plan.chronic: Return to sport protocol, dynamic stability, perturbation training.

name: Hip Osteoarthritis
description: Progressive degeneration of articular cartilage in the hip joint, associated with pain, stiffness, and loss of function.
key_findings: Groin pain, morning stiffness < 1 hour, loss of internal rotation and flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Pain with passive internal rotation. X-ray shows joint space narrowing, osteophytes.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs, gentle ROM (stationary bike).
treatment_plan.subacute: Hip abductor and external rotator strengthening, manual therapy (joint mobilizations).
treatment_plan.chronic: Weight management, assistive device if needed, ongoing strength and ROM program.

name: Acetabular Labral Tear
description: Tear of the labrum in the hip, often concurrent with Femoroacetabular Impingement (FAI). Types include anterior (most common) and posterior.
key_findings: Clicking, catching, or locking in the hip. Anterior groin pain exacerbated by hip flexion.
diagnostic_tips: FADIR test positive. MRA (MR Arthrogram) is the gold standard for labral pathology.

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid deep flexion/rotation, NSAIDs, intra-articular injection for diagnosis/relief.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbopelvic stabilization, gluteal strengthening, core control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Movement pattern retraining, avoid end-range pivoting under load.

name: Medial Meniscus Tear
description: Tear in the inner C-shaped fibrocartilage of the knee. Can be traumatic (younger) or degenerative (older).
causes: Twisting injury on a planted foot
key_findings: Joint line tenderness, episodes of knee catching or locking, delayed swelling.
diagnostic_tips: Thessaly test and McMurray's test. Pain localized tightly to the medial joint line.

treatment_plan.acute: Control effusion, restore ROM, quadriceps activation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Strengthening through pain-free range, proprioception.
treatment_plan.chronic: Agility drills if returning to sport, functionally specific loading.

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS) Type 1
description: Severe, continuous neuro-inflammatory pain condition that typically develops after a peripheral injury or surgery.
causes: Minor trauma without obvious nerve injury
key_findings: Allodynia, hyperalgesia, temperature asymmetry, vasomotor changes (skin color/sweating), trophic changes (hair/nails).
diagnostic_tips: Follow Budapest Criteria. Rule out local infection or DVT.

treatment_plan.acute: Desensitization, gentle AROM, mirror visual feedback therapy, avoid aggressive PROM.
treatment_plan.subacute: Graded motor imagery, stress loading (scrub and carry), TENS.
treatment_plan.chronic: Psychological support, pain neuroscience education, functional restoration.

key_findings: Unilateral arm pain, numbness in a dermatomal pattern, weakness in myotome, decreased reflexes.
diagnostic_tips: Spurling's test positive, relief with cervical distraction, MRI to confirm root compression.

treatment_plan.acute: Pain management, cervical collar (briefly), gentle ROM, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Cervical traction, deep neck flexor strengthening, nerve gliding exercises.
treatment_plan.chronic: Postural correction, progressive resistive exercises, ergonomics.

name: Rotator Cuff Tear (Variant 17)
description: A tear in one or more of the tendons of the rotator cuff muscles, most commonly the supraspinatus. Can be partial or full thickness.
key_findings: Pain over lateral deltoid, weakness with abduction and external rotation, night pain.
diagnostic_tips: Drop arm sign positive, painful arc, weakness on empty can test. MRI is gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling for comfort, cryotherapy, pendulum exercises, avoid active abduction.
treatment_plan.subacute: AAROM, isometrics for intact muscles, scapular stabilization.
treatment_plan.chronic: Isotonic strengthening (open and closed chain), plyometrics if returning to sports.

name: Lumbar Spondylolisthesis (Variant 17)
description: Forward translation of one vertebral body over the adjacent vertebra below, most commonly at L4-L5 or L5-S1. Often causes mechanical low back pain or neurogenic claudication.
key_findings: Step-off deformity on palpation, pain exacerbated by extension, relief with flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Lateral lumbar X-ray (flexion/extension views to assess instability).

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid extension, NSAIDs, core bracing if severe.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbar stabilization exercises (bias flexion), hamstring stretching.
treatment_plan.chronic: Core endurance training, functional activity modification.

name: Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) Tear (Variant 17)
description: Rupture of the ACL, a key stabilizing ligament of the knee, typically from a non-contact pivoting injury.
key_findings: Audible 'pop', immediate swelling (hemarthrosis), feeling of instability or 'giving way'.
diagnostic_tips: Lachman test is highly sensitive for acute tears. MRI for confirmation and meniscal involvement.

treatment_plan.acute: RICE, crutches, ROM restoration (especially extension), quad activation (NMES).
treatment_plan.subacute: Closed kinetic chain exercises, proprioceptive training, normalizing gait.
treatment_plan.chronic: Return to sport protocol, dynamic stability, perturbation training.

name: Hip Osteoarthritis (Variant 17)
description: Progressive degeneration of articular cartilage in the hip joint, associated with pain, stiffness, and loss of function.
key_findings: Groin pain, morning stiffness < 1 hour, loss of internal rotation and flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Pain with passive internal rotation. X-ray shows joint space narrowing, osteophytes.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs, gentle ROM (stationary bike).
treatment_plan.subacute: Hip abductor and external rotator strengthening, manual therapy (joint mobilizations).
treatment_plan.chronic: Weight management, assistive device if needed, ongoing strength and ROM program.

name: Acetabular Labral Tear (Variant 17)
description: Tear of the labrum in the hip, often concurrent with Femoroacetabular Impingement (FAI). Types include anterior (most common) and posterior.
key_findings: Clicking, catching, or locking in the hip. Anterior groin pain exacerbated by hip flexion.
diagnostic_tips: FADIR test positive. MRA (MR Arthrogram) is the gold standard for labral pathology.

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid deep flexion/rotation, NSAIDs, intra-articular injection for diagnosis/relief.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbopelvic stabilization, gluteal strengthening, core control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Movement pattern retraining, avoid end-range pivoting under load.

name: Medial Meniscus Tear (Variant 17)
description: Tear in the inner C-shaped fibrocartilage of the knee. Can be traumatic (younger) or degenerative (older).
causes: Twisting injury on a planted foot
key_findings: Joint line tenderness, episodes of knee catching or locking, delayed swelling.
diagnostic_tips: Thessaly test and McMurray's test. Pain localized tightly to the medial joint line.

treatment_plan.acute: Control effusion, restore ROM, quadriceps activation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Strengthening through pain-free range, proprioception.
treatment_plan.chronic: Agility drills if returning to sport, functionally specific loading.

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS) Type 1 (Variant 17)
description: Severe, continuous neuro-inflammatory pain condition that typically develops after a peripheral injury or surgery.
causes: Minor trauma without obvious nerve injury
key_findings: Allodynia, hyperalgesia, temperature asymmetry, vasomotor changes (skin color/sweating), trophic changes (hair/nails).
diagnostic_tips: Follow Budapest Criteria. Rule out local infection or DVT.

treatment_plan.acute: Desensitization, gentle AROM, mirror visual feedback therapy, avoid aggressive PROM.
treatment_plan.subacute: Graded motor imagery, stress loading (scrub and carry), TENS.
treatment_plan.chronic: Psychological support, pain neuroscience education, functional restoration.

key_findings: Clicking, catching, or locking in the hip. Anterior groin pain exacerbated by hip flexion.
diagnostic_tips: FADIR test positive. MRA (MR Arthrogram) is the gold standard for labral pathology.

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid deep flexion/rotation, NSAIDs, intra-articular injection for diagnosis/relief.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbopelvic stabilization, gluteal strengthening, core control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Movement pattern retraining, avoid end-range pivoting under load.

name: Medial Meniscus Tear (Variant 28)
description: Tear in the inner C-shaped fibrocartilage of the knee. Can be traumatic (younger) or degenerative (older).
causes: Twisting injury on a planted foot
key_findings: Joint line tenderness, episodes of knee catching or locking, delayed swelling.
diagnostic_tips: Thessaly test and McMurray's test. Pain localized tightly to the medial joint line.

treatment_plan.acute: Control effusion, restore ROM, quadriceps activation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Strengthening through pain-free range, proprioception.
treatment_plan.chronic: Agility drills if returning to sport, functionally specific loading.

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS) Type 1 (Variant 28)
description: Severe, continuous neuro-inflammatory pain condition that typically develops after a peripheral injury or surgery.
causes: Minor trauma without obvious nerve injury
key_findings: Allodynia, hyperalgesia, temperature asymmetry, vasomotor changes (skin color/sweating), trophic changes (hair/nails).
diagnostic_tips: Follow Budapest Criteria. Rule out local infection or DVT.

treatment_plan.acute: Desensitization, gentle AROM, mirror visual feedback therapy, avoid aggressive PROM.
treatment_plan.subacute: Graded motor imagery, stress loading (scrub and carry), TENS.
treatment_plan.chronic: Psychological support, pain neuroscience education, functional restoration.

name: Cervical Radiculopathy (Variant 29)
description: Compression or irritation of a cervical nerve root, commonly causing pain, numbness, or weakness in the upper extremity. Often due to disc herniation or spondylosis.
key_findings: Unilateral arm pain, numbness in a dermatomal pattern, weakness in myotome, decreased reflexes.
diagnostic_tips: Spurling's test positive, relief with cervical distraction, MRI to confirm root compression.

treatment_plan.acute: Pain management, cervical collar (briefly), gentle ROM, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Cervical traction, deep neck flexor strengthening, nerve gliding exercises.
treatment_plan.chronic: Postural correction, progressive resistive exercises, ergonomics.

name: Rotator Cuff Tear (Variant 29)
description: A tear in one or more of the tendons of the rotator cuff muscles, most commonly the supraspinatus. Can be partial or full thickness.
key_findings: Pain over lateral deltoid, weakness with abduction and external rotation, night pain.
diagnostic_tips: Drop arm sign positive, painful arc, weakness on empty can test. MRI is gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling for comfort, cryotherapy, pendulum exercises, avoid active abduction.
treatment_plan.subacute: AAROM, isometrics for intact muscles, scapular stabilization.
treatment_plan.chronic: Isotonic strengthening (open and closed chain), plyometrics if returning to sports.

name: Lumbar Spondylolisthesis (Variant 29)
description: Forward translation of one vertebral body over the adjacent vertebra below, most commonly at L4-L5 or L5-S1. Often causes mechanical low back pain or neurogenic claudication.
key_findings: Step-off deformity on palpation, pain exacerbated by extension, relief with flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Lateral lumbar X-ray (flexion/extension views to assess instability).

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid extension, NSAIDs, core bracing if severe.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbar stabilization exercises (bias flexion), hamstring stretching.
treatment_plan.chronic: Core endurance training, functional activity modification.

name: Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) Tear (Variant 29)
description: Rupture of the ACL, a key stabilizing ligament of the knee, typically from a non-contact pivoting injury.
key_findings: Audible 'pop', immediate swelling (hemarthrosis), feeling of instability or 'giving way'.
diagnostic_tips: Lachman test is highly sensitive for acute tears. MRI for confirmation and meniscal involvement.

treatment_plan.acute: RICE, crutches, ROM restoration (especially extension), quad activation (NMES).
treatment_plan.subacute: Closed kinetic chain exercises, proprioceptive training, normalizing gait.
treatment_plan.chronic: Return to sport protocol, dynamic stability, perturbation training.

name: Hip Osteoarthritis (Variant 29)
description: Progressive degeneration of articular cartilage in the hip joint, associated with pain, stiffness, and loss of function.
key_findings: Groin pain, morning stiffness < 1 hour, loss of internal rotation and flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Pain with passive internal rotation. X-ray shows joint space narrowing, osteophytes.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs, gentle ROM (stationary bike).
treatment_plan.subacute: Hip abductor and external rotator strengthening, manual therapy (joint mobilizations).
treatment_plan.chronic: Weight management, assistive device if needed, ongoing strength and ROM program.

treatment_plan.chronic: Postural correction, progressive resistive exercises, ergonomics.

name: Rotator Cuff Tear (Variant 104)
description: A tear in one or more of the tendons of the rotator cuff muscles, most commonly the supraspinatus. Can be partial or full thickness.
key_findings: Pain over lateral deltoid, weakness with abduction and external rotation, night pain.
diagnostic_tips: Drop arm sign positive, painful arc, weakness on empty can test. MRI is gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling for comfort, cryotherapy, pendulum exercises, avoid active abduction.
treatment_plan.subacute: AAROM, isometrics for intact muscles, scapular stabilization.
treatment_plan.chronic: Isotonic strengthening (open and closed chain), plyometrics if returning to sports.

name: Lumbar Spondylolisthesis (Variant 104)
description: Forward translation of one vertebral body over the adjacent vertebra below, most commonly at L4-L5 or L5-S1. Often causes mechanical low back pain or neurogenic claudication.
key_findings: Step-off deformity on palpation, pain exacerbated by extension, relief with flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Lateral lumbar X-ray (flexion/extension views to assess instability).

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid extension, NSAIDs, core bracing if severe.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbar stabilization exercises (bias flexion), hamstring stretching.
treatment_plan.chronic: Core endurance training, functional activity modification.

name: Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) Tear (Variant 104)
description: Rupture of the ACL, a key stabilizing ligament of the knee, typically from a non-contact pivoting injury.
key_findings: Audible 'pop', immediate swelling (hemarthrosis), feeling of instability or 'giving way'.
diagnostic_tips: Lachman test is highly sensitive for acute tears. MRI for confirmation and meniscal involvement.

treatment_plan.acute: RICE, crutches, ROM restoration (especially extension), quad activation (NMES).
treatment_plan.subacute: Closed kinetic chain exercises, proprioceptive training, normalizing gait.
treatment_plan.chronic: Return to sport protocol, dynamic stability, perturbation training.

name: Hip Osteoarthritis (Variant 104)
description: Progressive degeneration of articular cartilage in the hip joint, associated with pain, stiffness, and loss of function.
key_findings: Groin pain, morning stiffness < 1 hour, loss of internal rotation and flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Pain with passive internal rotation. X-ray shows joint space narrowing, osteophytes.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs, gentle ROM (stationary bike).
treatment_plan.subacute: Hip abductor and external rotator strengthening, manual therapy (joint mobilizations).
treatment_plan.chronic: Weight management, assistive device if needed, ongoing strength and ROM program.

name: Acetabular Labral Tear (Variant 104)
description: Tear of the labrum in the hip, often concurrent with Femoroacetabular Impingement (FAI). Types include anterior (most common) and posterior.
key_findings: Clicking, catching, or locking in the hip. Anterior groin pain exacerbated by hip flexion.
diagnostic_tips: FADIR test positive. MRA (MR Arthrogram) is the gold standard for labral pathology.

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid deep flexion/rotation, NSAIDs, intra-articular injection for diagnosis/relief.
treatment_plan.subacute: Lumbopelvic stabilization, gluteal strengthening, core control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Movement pattern retraining, avoid end-range pivoting under load.

name: Medial Meniscus Tear (Variant 104)
description: Tear in the inner C-shaped fibrocartilage of the knee. Can be traumatic (younger) or degenerative (older).
causes: Twisting injury on a planted foot
key_findings: Joint line tenderness, episodes of knee catching or locking, delayed swelling.
diagnostic_tips: Thessaly test and McMurray's test. Pain localized tightly to the medial joint line.

treatment_plan.acute: Control effusion, restore ROM, quadriceps activation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Strengthening through pain-free range, proprioception.
treatment_plan.chronic: Agility drills if returning to sport, functionally specific loading.

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS) Type 1 (Variant 104)
description: Severe, continuous neuro-inflammatory pain condition that typically develops after a peripheral injury or surgery.
causes: Minor trauma without obvious nerve injury
key_findings: Allodynia, hyperalgesia, temperature asymmetry, vasomotor changes (skin color/sweating), trophic changes (hair/nails).
diagnostic_tips: Follow Budapest Criteria. Rule out local infection or DVT.

treatment_plan.acute: Desensitization, gentle AROM, mirror visual feedback therapy, avoid aggressive PROM.
treatment_plan.subacute: Graded motor imagery, stress loading (scrub and carry), TENS.
treatment_plan.chronic: Psychological support, pain neuroscience education, functional restoration.

name: Lateral Epicondylalgia (Tennis Elbow)
description: Overuse tendinopathy of common extensor origin (ECRB primarily) at lateral epicondyle.
key_findings: Pain over lateral epicondyle, pain on resisted wrist extension and middle finger extension.
diagnostic_tips: Cozen's, Mill's, Maudsley's tests positive. US shows tendon thickening.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest, ice, NSAIDs, counterforce brace.
treatment_plan.subacute: Isometric wrist extensor loading, soft-tissue mobilization, MWM elbow.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy slow resistance / eccentric Tyler twist progression, ergonomic correction.

differential_diagnosis: Radial tunnel syndrome, C6 radiculopathy, PIN entrapment

name: Medial Epicondylalgia (Golfer's Elbow)
description: Tendinopathy of common flexor-pronator origin at medial epicondyle.
key_findings: Pain at medial epicondyle, pain on resisted wrist flexion/pronation, weak grip.
diagnostic_tips: Reverse Cozen's positive, palpation tender 5-10 mm distal to medial epicondyle.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, ice, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Isometric flexor loading, MWM, gentle stretching.
treatment_plan.chronic: Eccentric loading, scapular/grip retraining.

differential_diagnosis: Ulnar neuritis, MCL elbow sprain, C7 radiculopathy

name: Distal Biceps Tendon Rupture
description: Avulsion of distal biceps from radial tuberosity, usually traumatic eccentric overload.
key_findings: Audible pop, antecubital pain, ecchymosis, 'Popeye' deformity, weak supination > flexion.
diagnostic_tips: Hook test positive, MRI confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, ice, urgent ortho referral for surgical repair within 2-3 weeks.
treatment_plan.subacute: Post-op AROM 0-6 wks, gradual flexion/supination loading.
treatment_plan.chronic: Progressive resistance, return to lifting at 4-6 months.

red_flags: Inability to flex elbow against gravity

name: Olecranon Bursitis
description: Inflammation of subcutaneous bursa over olecranon process.
key_findings: Discrete fluctuant swelling at olecranon, ROM usually preserved unless septic.
diagnostic_tips: Aspiration if septic suspected (warmth, fever).

treatment_plan.acute: Compression, padding, NSAIDs; aspiration if septic.
treatment_plan.subacute: Avoid leaning on elbow, gentle ROM.
treatment_plan.chronic: Bursectomy if recurrent.

name: Ulnar Collateral Ligament Sprain (Elbow)
description: Sprain of medial UCL of elbow, common in overhead throwers.
key_findings: Medial elbow pain in late cocking/acceleration, decreased throwing velocity.
diagnostic_tips: Moving Valgus Stress Test, Milking Maneuver positive; MRI gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Throwing cessation, ice, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scapular/RTC strengthening, kinetic chain work.
treatment_plan.chronic: Interval throwing program; UCL reconstruction (Tommy John) for high-grade tears in pitchers.

name: Cubital Tunnel Syndrome
description: Compression of ulnar nerve at the cubital tunnel.
key_findings: Numbness in 4th/5th digits, weak grip, intrinsic atrophy in chronic cases.
diagnostic_tips: Tinel's at cubital tunnel, Elbow Flexion Test, NCS confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: Night extension splinting, activity modification, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Nerve gliding, ergonomic education.
treatment_plan.chronic: In-situ decompression or ulnar nerve transposition if conservative fails.

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
description: Median nerve compression at the carpal tunnel.
key_findings: Nocturnal paresthesia in median distribution, thenar weakness/atrophy in advanced cases.
diagnostic_tips: Phalen's, Tinel's, Carpal Compression Test; NCS gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Night cock-up splint, NSAIDs, ergonomic correction.
treatment_plan.subacute: Nerve/tendon gliding, activity modification, US-guided steroid.
treatment_plan.chronic: Endoscopic or open carpal tunnel release.

name: Guyon's Canal Syndrome
description: Compression of ulnar nerve in Guyon's canal at wrist.
key_findings: Sensory loss volar 4th/5th digits (sparing dorsum), interosseous weakness.
diagnostic_tips: Allen test, NCS, MRI for space-occupying lesion.

treatment_plan.acute: Padded gloves, ergonomic correction, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Nerve gliding.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical release if persistent.

name: Trigger Finger (Stenosing Flexor Tenosynovitis)
description: Mismatch between flexor tendon and A1 pulley causing catching/locking.
key_findings: Painful clicking, locking in flexion, palpable nodule at A1 pulley.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical exam; US for thickened pulley.

treatment_plan.acute: Splinting MCP in extension, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Tendon gliding, soft tissue work.
treatment_plan.chronic: Corticosteroid injection; A1 pulley release if persistent.

name: TFCC Tear
description: Triangular fibrocartilage complex tear at ulnar wrist (Palmer 1A-2E classification).
key_findings: Ulnar wrist pain, clicking on rotation, weak grip.
diagnostic_tips: Press test, TFCC grind, MR arthrogram gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Ulnar gutter splint 4-6 weeks, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Forearm/wrist proprioception, grip retraining.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic debridement or repair if persistent.

name: Scapholunate Ligament Tear
description: Disruption of scapholunate interosseous ligament - most common carpal instability.
key_findings: Dorsal wrist pain, tenderness over SL interval, weak grip.
diagnostic_tips: Watson scaphoid shift test positive; X-ray shows Terry Thomas sign (>3mm gap).

treatment_plan.acute: Immobilization, ortho referral.
treatment_plan.subacute: Post-op protected ROM.
treatment_plan.chronic: Open repair / capsulodesis / SLAC reconstruction depending on chronicity.

name: Scaphoid Fracture
description: Fracture of scaphoid bone, high non-union risk due to retrograde blood supply.
key_findings: Snuffbox tenderness, scaphoid tubercle tenderness, pain on axial thumb compression.
diagnostic_tips: X-ray (may be initially negative) - MRI/CT for occult fracture.

treatment_plan.acute: Thumb spica cast 6-12 weeks; ORIF for displaced or proximal pole.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive ROM, isometric loading.
treatment_plan.chronic: Bone graft/Russe procedure for non-union.

red_flags: Persistent snuffbox pain >2 wks despite normal X-ray

name: Colles' Fracture
description: Distal radius fracture with dorsal angulation, classic 'dinner-fork' deformity.
key_findings: Wrist pain, swelling, dorsal angulation, limited ROM.
diagnostic_tips: X-ray confirms; CT for intra-articular involvement.

treatment_plan.acute: Closed reduction + cast 4-6 weeks; ORIF if unstable.
treatment_plan.subacute: Wrist/forearm ROM, edema control, scar management.
treatment_plan.chronic: Progressive strengthening, grip work, dexterity.

red_flags: Median nerve symptoms (acute carpal tunnel post-reduction)

name: Smith's Fracture
description: Reverse Colles' - distal radius fracture with volar angulation.
key_findings: Volar angulation 'garden spade' deformity.
diagnostic_tips: X-ray; usually requires ORIF due to instability.

treatment_plan.acute: ORIF preferred.
treatment_plan.subacute: Wrist ROM, edema control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Strength + functional retraining.

name: Boxer's Fracture (5th Metacarpal Neck)
description: Fracture of 5th metacarpal neck, classically from punching with closed fist.
key_findings: Dorsal hand swelling, loss of knuckle prominence, angulation.
diagnostic_tips: X-ray; acceptable angulation up to 40 degrees.

treatment_plan.acute: Ulnar gutter splint 3-4 weeks; reduction if >40 degrees angulation.
treatment_plan.subacute: AROM hand/wrist, edema control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Grip strengthening, functional training.

name: Mallet Finger
description: Avulsion of terminal extensor tendon at distal phalanx.
key_findings: Inability to actively extend DIP; flexed DIP at rest.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical; X-ray to assess bony avulsion.

treatment_plan.acute: DIP extension splint 6-8 weeks continuously.
treatment_plan.subacute: Gradual DIP flexion, splint at night.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgery if large bony fragment with subluxation.

name: Boutonniere Deformity
description: Disruption of central slip of extensor tendon causing PIP flexion + DIP hyperextension.
key_findings: Inability to actively extend PIP; characteristic deformity over weeks.
diagnostic_tips: Elson's test positive.

treatment_plan.acute: PIP extension splinting (DIP free) 6 weeks.
treatment_plan.subacute: Active DIP flexion exercises.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical reconstruction if fixed.

name: Swan Neck Deformity
description: PIP hyperextension + DIP flexion deformity, common in RA.
key_findings: Characteristic finger posture; functional grip impairment.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical.

treatment_plan.acute: Splinting in slight PIP flexion.
treatment_plan.subacute: Hand therapy, joint protection.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical correction (Littler procedure).

name: Dupuytren's Contracture
description: Progressive palmar fibromatosis causing flexion contracture of fingers.
key_findings: Palmar nodules then cords causing MCP/PIP flexion contracture, ring/little finger most common.
diagnostic_tips: Hueston tabletop test positive.

treatment_plan.acute: Observation; hand therapy.
treatment_plan.subacute: Stretching, splinting.
treatment_plan.chronic: Collagenase injection, needle aponeurotomy, fasciectomy.

name: Lateral Ankle Sprain (ATFL)
description: Inversion injury tearing anterior talofibular ligament - most common ankle injury.
key_findings: Lateral ankle pain, swelling, bruising, antalgic gait.
diagnostic_tips: Anterior drawer, talar tilt; Ottawa rules to exclude fracture.

treatment_plan.acute: PEACE & LOVE protocol; bracing; weight-bear as tolerated.
treatment_plan.subacute: Proprioceptive (BAPS/wobble), peroneal strengthening, MWM ankle.
treatment_plan.chronic: Plyometrics, agility, return-to-sport criteria.

name: Syndesmotic 'High Ankle' Sprain
description: Sprain of distal tibiofibular syndesmosis.
key_findings: Pain proximal to ankle joint line on push-off, longer recovery than lateral sprain.
diagnostic_tips: Squeeze test, Kleiger external rotation test; X-ray to assess diastasis.

treatment_plan.acute: Boot/non-weight bearing 1-3 wks; ORIF if diastasis.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive WB, ROM, syndesmosis taping.
treatment_plan.chronic: Sport-specific reconditioning at 6-12 wks.

name: Achilles Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative midportion or insertional Achilles tendinosis.
key_findings: Morning stiffness, pain 2-6 cm above insertion, palpable thickening.
diagnostic_tips: Royal London hospital test, US/MRI for tendon thickening.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, ice, NSAIDs, heel lift.
treatment_plan.subacute: Isometric calf loading; gradual eccentric heel drops (Alfredson).
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy slow resistance, progressive loading; PRP/surgery if refractory.

name: Achilles Tendon Rupture
description: Complete Achilles rupture, classic in 30-50yo 'weekend warrior'.
key_findings: Audible pop, palpable gap, weak plantarflexion, antalgic gait.
diagnostic_tips: Thompson test positive; MRI/US confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: Boot in equinus or surgical repair within 2 wks.
treatment_plan.subacute: Functional rehab with progressive dorsiflexion 0-12 wks.
treatment_plan.chronic: Plyometrics, return-to-sport at 6-9 months.

name: Plantar Fasciitis
description: Degenerative tendinopathy of plantar fascia at calcaneal origin.
key_findings: First-step morning heel pain, pain at medial calcaneal tubercle.
diagnostic_tips: Windlass test positive; US shows fascia >4 mm.

treatment_plan.acute: Ice, NSAIDs, gentle stretching, taping.
treatment_plan.subacute: Plantar fascia/calf stretching, intrinsic foot strengthening, night splint.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy slow resistance with toes extended; ESWT; orthotics.

name: Tarsal Tunnel Syndrome
description: Posterior tibial nerve compression at medial ankle.
key_findings: Burning/tingling sole of foot, worse with prolonged standing.
diagnostic_tips: Tinel's at tarsal tunnel, dorsiflexion-eversion test; NCS confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs, orthotics.
treatment_plan.subacute: Nerve gliding, soft tissue release.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical release if conservative fails.

name: Morton's Neuroma
description: Perineural fibrosis of common digital nerve, usually 3rd web space.
key_findings: Burning/sharp pain in forefoot radiating to toes; relieved by removing shoe.
diagnostic_tips: Mulder's click positive; US confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: Wider shoes, metatarsal pad, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Intrinsic foot work, footwear correction.
treatment_plan.chronic: Steroid/alcohol injection; neurectomy if refractory.

name: Hallux Valgus (Bunion)
description: Lateral deviation of great toe with medial 1st MTP eminence.
key_findings: Medial 1st MTP pain, deformity, callus.
diagnostic_tips: Weight-bearing X-ray for HV angle and intermetatarsal angle.

treatment_plan.acute: Footwear modification, toe spacers, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Intrinsic foot strengthening, mobilizations.
treatment_plan.chronic: Bunionectomy/osteotomy for severe deformity.

name: Hallux Rigidus
description: Osteoarthritis of 1st MTP with progressive dorsiflexion loss.
key_findings: Dorsal 1st MTP pain, osteophyte, limited dorsiflexion (<55 degrees).
diagnostic_tips: X-ray shows joint space loss + osteophytes.

treatment_plan.acute: Stiff-soled shoe / Morton extension orthotic, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Joint mobilization, gentle ROM.
treatment_plan.chronic: Cheilectomy or arthrodesis for advanced disease.

name: Posterior Tibial Tendon Dysfunction
description: Tendinopathy/tear of tibialis posterior leading to progressive flatfoot.
key_findings: Medial ankle pain/swelling, 'too many toes' sign, single-heel-rise weakness.
diagnostic_tips: Single heel rise test, US/MRI.

treatment_plan.acute: Boot 4-6 weeks, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Eccentric tib post strengthening, custom orthotic.
treatment_plan.chronic: Tendon transfer + medial calc osteotomy in advanced cases.

name: Sever's Disease (Calcaneal Apophysitis)
description: Traction apophysitis of calcaneal apophysis in skeletally immature athletes.
key_findings: Posterior heel pain, tender squeeze test, worse with running/jumping.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical; X-ray to exclude other causes.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, ice, heel cup.
treatment_plan.subacute: Calf stretching, eccentric loading.
treatment_plan.chronic: Resolves with skeletal maturity; gradual return to sport.

name: Lisfranc Injury
description: Disruption of tarsometatarsal ligament complex, often missed.
key_findings: Midfoot pain, swelling, plantar bruising, inability to bear weight.
diagnostic_tips: WB X-ray for diastasis between 1st-2nd metatarsal; CT/MRI confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: NWB cast 6-8 wks if non-displaced; ORIF if displaced.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive WB, ROM at 8-12 wks.
treatment_plan.chronic: Midfoot arthrodesis for severe injuries.

red_flags: Plantar ecchymosis = highly suggestive of Lisfranc

name: Costochondritis
description: Inflammation of costochondral junctions, benign chest wall pain.
key_findings: Reproducible chest wall tenderness, pain with deep breathing/twisting.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical diagnosis of exclusion; ECG/troponin to exclude cardiac.

treatment_plan.acute: NSAIDs, reassurance, relative rest.
treatment_plan.subacute: Thoracic mobility, postural correction.
treatment_plan.chronic: Manual therapy, breathing exercises.

name: Thoracic Outlet Syndrome (Neurogenic)
description: Compression of brachial plexus in scalene/costoclavicular/pec-minor space.
key_findings: Arm fatigue, paresthesia (often C8-T1), worse with overhead activity.
diagnostic_tips: Roos / EAST test, Adson's, costoclavicular maneuver; EMG for confirmation.

treatment_plan.acute: Postural education, pec/scalene release.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scapular stabilization, nerve gliding.
treatment_plan.chronic: First rib resection if refractory.

name: Scheuermann's Kyphosis
description: Rigid thoracic kyphosis from anterior wedging of >=3 contiguous vertebrae.
key_findings: Postural pain, fixed kyphosis >45 degrees, anterior wedging on X-ray.
diagnostic_tips: Lateral X-ray showing wedging >=5 degrees in 3 vertebrae.

treatment_plan.acute: Postural and ergonomic education.
treatment_plan.subacute: Thoracic extension exercises, core strengthening.
treatment_plan.chronic: Bracing if curve progressing; surgery for >75 degrees.

name: Pubic Symphysis Dysfunction
description: Pain and instability at pubic symphysis, common postpartum or in athletes.
key_findings: Anterior pelvic pain, waddling gait, pain with single-leg stance.
diagnostic_tips: Active straight leg raise, palpation tenderness; X-ray for separation.

treatment_plan.acute: Pelvic belt, relative rest, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Pelvic floor + deep abdominal activation, glute work.
treatment_plan.chronic: Progressive loading, return to sport criteria.

name: Sacroiliac Joint Dysfunction
description: Pain originating from SIJ - capsular, ligamentous, or arthritic.
key_findings: Unilateral PSIS pain, pain on transitional movements.
diagnostic_tips: >=3 of 5 SIJ provocation tests positive (Laslett cluster).

treatment_plan.acute: NSAIDs, SIJ belt, relative rest.
treatment_plan.subacute: SIJ MET, glute med strengthening, motor control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Image-guided steroid; RFA for chronic; arthrodesis rare.

name: Coccydynia
description: Pain at coccyx, often post-traumatic or post-partum.
key_findings: Pain on sitting, relieved by standing; tender coccyx.
diagnostic_tips: Dynamic X-ray (sitting vs standing) shows hypermobility.

treatment_plan.acute: Cushion (donut/wedge), NSAIDs, ice.
treatment_plan.subacute: Pelvic floor down-training, manual mobilization.
treatment_plan.chronic: Steroid injection; coccygectomy if refractory.

name: TMJ Disc Displacement with Reduction
description: Anterior displacement of TMJ disc that reduces during opening (clicking).
key_findings: Reciprocal click on opening/closing, intermittent locking.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical exam; MRI for confirmation.

treatment_plan.acute: Soft diet, NSAIDs, jaw rest.
treatment_plan.subacute: TMJ joint mobilization, posture, jaw kinematic re-education.
treatment_plan.chronic: Splint therapy; arthrocentesis if persistent.

name: TMJ Disc Displacement without Reduction (Closed Lock)
description: Anterior disc displacement that does NOT reduce - restricted opening.
key_findings: Sudden limited mouth opening (<25 mm), deflection to affected side.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical + MRI.

treatment_plan.acute: Manual disc reduction, soft diet, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Mobilization, motor control of jaw.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthrocentesis or arthroscopy.

name: TMJ Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative arthritis of TMJ.
key_findings: Chronic dull pain, crepitus, limited opening, morning stiffness.
diagnostic_tips: CBCT shows joint surface changes.

treatment_plan.acute: NSAIDs, soft diet, splint.
treatment_plan.subacute: TMJ mobilization, motor control, postural education.
treatment_plan.chronic: Intra-articular injections; arthroplasty rarely.

name: Cellulitis
description: Bacterial skin/subcutaneous infection.
key_findings: Warm, erythematous, tender, ill-defined border, +/- fever.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical; blood cultures if systemic.

treatment_plan.acute: Empiric antibiotics, elevation, mark border.
treatment_plan.subacute: Compression for residual edema.
treatment_plan.chronic: Identify and treat underlying portal of entry.

red_flags: Rapidly spreading erythema, hypotension - necrotizing fasciitis

name: Necrotizing Fasciitis
description: Surgical emergency - rapidly spreading deep soft-tissue infection.
key_findings: Pain out of proportion, dusky skin, crepitus, systemic toxicity.
diagnostic_tips: LRINEC score, surgical exploration.

treatment_plan.acute: IMMEDIATE surgical debridement + broad-spectrum IV antibiotics.
treatment_plan.subacute: Wound care, dressing changes, contracture prevention.
treatment_plan.chronic: Reconstruction, scar management, functional rehab.

name: Pressure Ulcer (Stage I-IV)
description: Tissue damage from sustained pressure over bony prominence.
key_findings: Stage I non-blanchable erythema to Stage IV bone/tendon exposure.
diagnostic_tips: Braden scale risk; clinical staging.

treatment_plan.acute: Off-loading, dressing per stage, treat infection.
treatment_plan.subacute: Wound care, nutrition, repositioning q2h.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical flap for stage IV; long-term prevention strategy.

name: Hypertrophic Scar
description: Raised scar confined to original wound border.
key_findings: Red, raised, pruritic scar within wound margins.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical; Vancouver Scar Scale.

treatment_plan.acute: Silicone sheets, pressure garments early.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scar massage, stretching, intralesional steroid.
treatment_plan.chronic: Laser, surgical revision.

name: Lymphedema (Secondary)
description: Chronic interstitial fluid accumulation, often post-cancer treatment.
key_findings: Pitting then non-pitting limb edema, Stemmer's sign positive in chronic.
diagnostic_tips: Stemmer's sign, bioimpedance, lymphoscintigraphy.

treatment_plan.acute: Skin care, education, baseline measurements.
treatment_plan.subacute: Complete decongestive therapy: MLD + compression bandaging + exercise + skin care.
treatment_plan.chronic: Long-term compression garments, intermittent pneumatic compression.

name: Burn - Partial Thickness
description: Burn affecting epidermis and partial dermis.
key_findings: Painful, blistered, blanching erythema.
diagnostic_tips: Rule of nines for TBSA; depth assessment.

treatment_plan.acute: Cooling, debridement, silver sulfadiazine, fluid resuscitation if >15% TBSA.
treatment_plan.subacute: Splinting in anti-deformity position, ROM, edema control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Scar management, contracture prevention, functional rehab.

name: SLAP Tear Type I (Fraying)
description: Degenerative fraying of superior labrum without detachment.
key_findings: Vague shoulder pain, mild crepitus.
diagnostic_tips: MR arthrogram; arthroscopy gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: NSAIDs, activity modification.
treatment_plan.subacute: RTC + scapular strengthening.
treatment_plan.chronic: Debridement if persistent.

name: SLAP Tear Type II (Detachment)
description: Detachment of biceps anchor from superior glenoid - most common SLAP.
key_findings: Deep shoulder pain, popping, pain with overhead activity.
diagnostic_tips: MR arthrogram, dynamic O'Brien, anterior slide test.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scapular and RTC strengthening.
treatment_plan.chronic: SLAP repair vs biceps tenodesis (preferred >35yo).

name: SLAP Tear Type III (Bucket Handle)
description: Bucket-handle tear of superior labrum with intact biceps anchor.
key_findings: Mechanical catching, pain.
diagnostic_tips: MR arthrogram; arthroscopy.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Conservative trial.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic debridement of bucket fragment.

name: SLAP Tear Type IV (Bucket Handle + Biceps Extension)
description: Bucket-handle tear extending into biceps tendon.
key_findings: Catching plus biceps pain.
diagnostic_tips: MR arthrogram.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Conservative trial.
treatment_plan.chronic: Repair labrum + biceps tenodesis or tenotomy.

name: Bankart Lesion (Anterior-Inferior Labral Tear)
description: Avulsion of anterior-inferior labrum after anterior shoulder dislocation.
key_findings: Recurrent instability, apprehension in abd/ER.
diagnostic_tips: Apprehension + Relocation test, MR arthrogram.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling 1-3 wks, ER strengthening.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive RTC and scapular work.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic Bankart repair if recurrent instability.

name: Bony Bankart Lesion
description: Bony fragment of anterior glenoid + labrum after dislocation.
key_findings: Glenoid bone loss, persistent instability.
diagnostic_tips: CT for glenoid bone loss quantification.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, immobilization.
treatment_plan.subacute: Strengthening.
treatment_plan.chronic: Latarjet procedure if >20-25% bone loss.

name: Hip Labral Tear (Anterior)
description: Tear of acetabular labrum, most commonly anterior.
key_findings: Anterior groin pain, C-sign, clicking, pain with pivoting.
diagnostic_tips: FADIR test, FABER; MR arthrogram.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Hip stabilization, motor control, MWM.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic labral repair + cam/pincer correction.

name: Hip Labral Tear (Posterior)
description: Posterior acetabular labral tear, less common; often from posterior impingement.
key_findings: Posterior hip pain, deep gluteal pain.
diagnostic_tips: Posterior impingement test; MR arthrogram.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification.
treatment_plan.subacute: Anterior chain work, hip control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic repair if persistent.

name: Snapping Hip Syndrome (Coxa Saltans)
description: Audible/palpable snap during hip motion, internal (iliopsoas over iliopectineal eminence) or external (IT band over greater trochanter).
key_findings: Reproducible snap with hip flexion-extension; usually painless but can become symptomatic.
diagnostic_tips: Dynamic ultrasound visualizes snapping structure.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Iliopsoas/IT band stretching, glute strengthening, motor control.
treatment_plan.chronic: Image-guided steroid; surgical lengthening rare.

name: Pressure Ulcer (Stage I-IV)
description: Localised tissue damage over bony prominence due to sustained pressure/shear, common in immobile patients.
key_findings: Non-blanchable erythema progressing to full-thickness skin loss with exposed muscle/bone in advanced stages.
diagnostic_tips: Use Braden Scale to predict risk; stage by NPUAP guidelines; image with consistent positioning.

treatment_plan.acute: Pressure offloading every 2 h, wound cleansing, moisture-balanced dressing, nutrition optimisation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Negative pressure wound therapy for stage III-IV, debridement of necrotic tissue, mobility progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical flap if non-healing >6 months; long-term seating/bed surface review; carer education.

name: Cellulitis (Lower Limb)
description: Acute bacterial infection of dermis and subcutaneous tissue, usually streptococcal or staphylococcal.
key_findings: Hot, red, tender, poorly demarcated swelling with systemic features (fever, malaise).
diagnostic_tips: Mark erythema border to track progression; differentiate from DVT (pain pattern, Wells score) and stasis dermatitis (chronic, bilateral).

treatment_plan.acute: Refer for IV/PO antibiotics, limb elevation, immobilisation initially; defer exercise.
treatment_plan.subacute: Once afebrile and erythema receding: graded mobilisation, compression for residual oedema.
treatment_plan.chronic: Address underlying skin barrier (treat tinea, manage lymphoedema, foot care education).

red_flags: Necrotising fasciitis (pain out of proportion, crepitus)

name: Stasis Dermatitis
description: Inflammatory skin change of lower leg secondary to chronic venous hypertension.
key_findings: Hyperpigmentation, scaling, weeping over medial gaiter area; often misdiagnosed as cellulitis.
diagnostic_tips: Bilateral and chronic — unlike unilateral acute cellulitis. Confirm with venous duplex.

treatment_plan.acute: Topical emollient, mild steroid for flare, leg elevation.
treatment_plan.subacute: Graded compression (20-30 mmHg if ABI ≥0.8), calf-pump exercise programme.
treatment_plan.chronic: Lifelong compression hosiery, weight management, structured walking programme.

name: Hidradenitis Suppurativa
description: Chronic inflammatory follicular skin disease affecting intertriginous areas.
key_findings: Recurrent painful nodules, abscesses, sinus tracts in axillae, groin, perineum.
diagnostic_tips: Hurley staging (I-III) guides management; differentiate from simple folliculitis or furunculosis.

treatment_plan.acute: Refer dermatology; warm compresses, avoid tight clothing.
treatment_plan.subacute: Address contributing factors: smoking cessation, weight management, gentle mobility for affected joints.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical excision for stage III; ROM exercises post-op to prevent contracture (esp. axilla).

name: Lipoedema
description: Symmetrical adipose tissue disorder of legs (and sometimes arms) with pain and easy bruising — distinct from obesity and lymphoedema.
key_findings: Bilateral symmetrical 'column-like' fat deposition sparing feet (cuff sign), tenderness to pressure.
diagnostic_tips: Stemmer's sign negative (vs lymphoedema). Bilateral symmetrical distribution sparing feet is pathognomonic.

treatment_plan.acute: Education, skin care, light compression if tolerated.
treatment_plan.subacute: Flat-knit graduated compression, low-impact aquatic exercise, lymphatic drainage.
treatment_plan.chronic: Tumescent liposuction in selected severe cases; lifelong compression and exercise.

name: Lymphoedema (Secondary)
description: Chronic interstitial fluid accumulation due to lymphatic insufficiency, often post node-dissection or radiotherapy.
key_findings: Unilateral non-pitting (chronic) swelling, positive Stemmer's sign, skin thickening.
diagnostic_tips: Stemmer's sign positive in lymphoedema. ISL Stage 0-III guides intensity of complete decongestive therapy.

treatment_plan.acute: Skin care, MLD initiation, education.
treatment_plan.subacute: Complete Decongestive Therapy: MLD + multilayer bandaging + exercise + skin care for 2-4 weeks.
treatment_plan.chronic: Maintenance: flat-knit garments, self-MLD, deep breathing, graduated exercise.

name: Burn Injury (Partial-Thickness)
description: Thermal/chemical/electrical injury to dermis with risk of contracture if joints involved.
key_findings: Painful red blistered skin, blanches with pressure; healing within 2-3 weeks if uncomplicated.
diagnostic_tips: Estimate %TBSA (rule of nines/Lund-Browder); refer if >10% adult, >5% child, face/hands/perineum or full-thickness.

treatment_plan.acute: Cool with running water 20 min, dressings, analgesia, splint joints in anti-deformity position.
treatment_plan.subacute: Active and AAROM within pain limits, scar massage once healed, pressure garments at maturation.
treatment_plan.chronic: Long-term scar management 12-24 months; address psychological sequelae, return to function.

name: Hypertrophic / Keloid Scar
description: Pathological scar response with excessive collagen deposition; keloid extends beyond original wound.
key_findings: Raised, red/dark, often itchy/painful scar; keloid grows beyond wound margins.
diagnostic_tips: Hypertrophic stays within wound borders; keloid extends beyond. Both can limit ROM if over a joint.

treatment_plan.acute: Wound healing optimisation, silicone sheet/gel, sun protection.
treatment_plan.subacute: Pressure garment 23 h/day, scar massage, mobilisation if joint involved.
treatment_plan.chronic: Intralesional steroid, laser, surgical revision with adjuvant therapy for refractory keloid.

red_flags: Suspected malignant change in chronic scar

name: Osteochondral Lesion of Talus (OLT)
description: Focal injury of talar dome cartilage and underlying subchondral bone, often post ankle sprain.
key_findings: Persistent deep ankle pain, catching, swelling after ankle sprain that fails to settle.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard; Berndt-Harty classification grades severity.

treatment_plan.acute: Boot immobilisation if acute, NSAIDs, offloading.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive ROM, peroneal strengthening, proprioception, gradual loading.
treatment_plan.chronic: Microfracture, OATS or autologous chondrocyte implantation if non-responsive.

name: Chondral Defect of Knee (Outerbridge II-III)
description: Focal cartilage damage of femoral condyle or trochlea, often traumatic or secondary to malalignment.
key_findings: Catching, locking, effusion after activity; often a discrete history.
diagnostic_tips: MRI with cartilage-sensitive sequences; arthroscopy for definitive grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, offloading brace, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Quad/glute strengthening, range restoration, low-impact aerobic.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical options: microfracture, MACI, osteotomy if malaligned.

name: Synovial Chondromatosis
description: Metaplastic formation of cartilaginous nodules within synovium, can detach as loose bodies.
key_findings: Mechanical symptoms (catching, locking), effusion, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Radiographs show calcified loose bodies; MRI confirms synovial origin.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: ROM exercises, quadriceps strengthening, avoid impact loading.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic removal of loose bodies and synovectomy.

name: Acetabular Labral Tear
description: Tear of fibrocartilaginous labrum surrounding acetabulum, common with FAI morphology.
key_findings: Anterior groin pain (C-sign), clicking, catching with hip flexion/IR.
diagnostic_tips: FADIR test sensitive; MR arthrogram is gold standard.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, NSAIDs, hip flexor downtraining.
treatment_plan.subacute: Hip stabilisers (glute med/min), motor control, avoid provocative end-range positions.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic labral repair/debridement with FAI correction if morphology present.

name: Glenoid Labral Tear — Type V SLAP
description: SLAP tear extending into anteroinferior labrum (Bankart-type extension).
key_findings: Anterior instability + biceps anchor symptoms; positive apprehension and SLAP signs.
diagnostic_tips: MR arthrogram superior to plain MRI for SLAP detection.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling for comfort, avoid provocative positions.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scapular and rotator cuff retraining; biceps load management.
treatment_plan.chronic: Arthroscopic SLAP repair + Bankart repair if instability persists.

name: Meralgia Paresthetica
description: Compression neuropathy of lateral femoral cutaneous nerve at inguinal ligament.
key_findings: Burning, numbness, tingling over anterolateral thigh; no motor deficit.
diagnostic_tips: Pelvic compression test reproduces symptoms; ultrasound-guided block confirms diagnosis.

treatment_plan.acute: Remove compressive factor (belt, clothing), weight management, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Soft-tissue mobilisation, neural mobilisation, postural retraining.
treatment_plan.chronic: Local anaesthetic ± steroid block; surgical decompression if refractory.

name: Tarsal Tunnel Syndrome
description: Posterior tibial nerve compression beneath flexor retinaculum at medial ankle.
key_findings: Burning medial foot pain radiating to sole, worse with standing/walking.
diagnostic_tips: Tinel's at tarsal tunnel; NCS confirms; MRI for space-occupying lesion.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, orthoses for pronation control, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Neural gliding, intrinsic foot strengthening, posterior tibialis training.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical decompression if conservative fails after 3-6 months.

name: Piriformis Syndrome
description: Sciatic nerve irritation by piriformis muscle, deep gluteal pain ± radicular features.
key_findings: Deep buttock pain reproduced by sitting/hip IR; may radiate down leg.
diagnostic_tips: FAIR test, Pace's sign; MRI to exclude lumbar pathology.

treatment_plan.acute: Avoid provocative sitting, NSAIDs, gentle stretching.
treatment_plan.subacute: Piriformis stretching, glute med strengthening, neural mobilisation.
treatment_plan.chronic: Image-guided injection; surgical release rare.

name: Saturday Night Palsy (Radial Nerve)
description: Radial nerve compression at spiral groove of humerus, classically after compressed sleep.
key_findings: Wrist drop, finger extensor weakness, sensory loss dorsal hand.
diagnostic_tips: EMG/NCS at 3 weeks; recovery typically over weeks-months if neuropraxia.

treatment_plan.acute: Cock-up wrist splint, avoid further compression, neural protection.
treatment_plan.subacute: Active assisted ROM, tendon gliding, sensory re-education.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical exploration if no recovery by 3-6 months or open injury.

name: Stress Fracture of Tibia
description: Microscopic bone failure from repetitive submaximal loading exceeding remodelling capacity.
key_findings: Localised pain on tibial border worse with impact, focal tenderness, eased by rest.
diagnostic_tips: MRI more sensitive than X-ray (which lags 2-4 weeks); BMD if recurrent.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity cessation, boot for high-risk sites, address energy availability.
treatment_plan.subacute: Cross-train (cycle/swim), graduated walk-jog programme once pain-free.
treatment_plan.chronic: Address training load, biomechanics, nutrition; bone density review if recurrent.

name: Osteitis Pubis
description: Inflammatory/overuse condition of pubic symphysis common in kicking sports.
key_findings: Anterior pelvic pain reproduced with adductor squeeze and resisted sit-up.
diagnostic_tips: Adductor squeeze test at 0/45/90°; MRI shows symphyseal oedema.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest, NSAIDs, address contributing biomechanics.
treatment_plan.subacute: Adductor strengthening (Holmich), core control, gradual return-to-running.
treatment_plan.chronic: Maintain conditioning; surgical curettage rare and last-line.

name: Os Trigonum Syndrome
description: Posterior ankle impingement from accessory ossicle posterior to talus, common in dancers.
key_findings: Posterior ankle pain with forced plantarflexion; relieved by avoidance.
diagnostic_tips: Forced plantarflexion test reproduces pain; lateral X-ray identifies ossicle; MRI confirms.

treatment_plan.acute: Activity modification, boot if severe, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Ankle mobility, calf flexibility, FHL gliding, return to dance progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Image-guided injection; arthroscopic excision if refractory.

name: Sternoclavicular Joint Sprain
description: Injury to SC joint capsule/ligaments from direct or indirect trauma.
key_findings: Localised SC pain, swelling, pain with overhead/horizontal adduction.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out posterior dislocation (vascular emergency) — urgent CT if suspected.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, ice, analgesia; URGENT CT if posterior dislocation suspected.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scapular and shoulder girdle strengthening, gradual return to overhead.
treatment_plan.chronic: Conservative for grade I-II; surgical reduction if posterior dislocation.

name: Acromioclavicular Joint Sprain
description: AC joint ligament/capsule injury graded I-VI (Rockwood) by displacement.
key_findings: Pain over AC joint, deformity in higher grades, painful cross-body adduction.
diagnostic_tips: Rockwood I-VI; weight-bearing X-ray for subtle separation.

treatment_plan.acute: Sling, ice, analgesia, early scapular setting.
treatment_plan.subacute: Scapular stabiliser progression, rotator cuff loading, AROM in pain-free range.
treatment_plan.chronic: Conservative for I-III; surgical for IV-VI or persistent symptomatic III.

name: Sacroiliac Joint Dysfunction
description: Pain arising from SI joint, often unilateral over PSIS, mechanical or inflammatory.
key_findings: Unilateral pain below L5, ≥3 positive provocation tests increase likelihood.
diagnostic_tips: Cluster of Laslett (≥3/5 positive) has good diagnostic utility.

treatment_plan.acute: Pelvic belt for symptom modulation, NSAIDs, education.
treatment_plan.subacute: Motor control of pelvic stabilisers, glute strengthening, manual therapy.
treatment_plan.chronic: Address contributors (leg-length, pregnancy biomechanics); injection if refractory.

name: Spring Ligament (Plantar Calcaneonavicular) Sprain
description: Plantar calcaneonavicular ligament insufficiency contributing to acquired flatfoot.
key_findings: Medial midfoot pain, navicular drop, single-heel-rise weakness.
diagnostic_tips: MRI to assess integrity; correlate with tib post function.

treatment_plan.acute: Offloading orthoses (medial post), boot if acute, NSAIDs.
treatment_plan.subacute: Tibialis posterior strengthening, intrinsic foot training, calf flexibility.
treatment_plan.chronic: Surgical reconstruction if progressive deformity.

name: Lisfranc Ligament Injury
description: Disruption of tarsometatarsal joint complex — easily missed and high morbidity if untreated.
key_findings: Midfoot pain, plantar bruising, inability to weight-bear, gap between 1st-2nd MT.
diagnostic_tips: Weight-bearing X-ray; CT for subtle injuries; high index of suspicion needed.

treatment_plan.acute: NWB, boot/cast, urgent orthopaedic review.
treatment_plan.subacute: Stable injuries: graduated WB, intrinsic strengthening, gait re-education.
treatment_plan.chronic: Unstable: ORIF or primary fusion; long-term arch support.

name: Hamstring Strain — Type I (Sprinting Type)
description: High-speed running injury at proximal biceps femoris musculotendinous junction.
key_findings: Sudden posterior thigh pain mid-sprint, palpable defect possible, weakness in hip extension.
diagnostic_tips: British Athletics Muscle Injury Classification (0-IV); MRI guides return-to-play.

treatment_plan.acute: POLICE, crutches if antalgic, isometric loading from day 1.
treatment_plan.subacute: Eccentric loading (Nordic, Askling L-protocol), running progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Maintenance Nordic programme, address kinetic chain, criteria-based RTP.

red_flags: Complete proximal avulsion (palpable defect, severe weakness)

name: Calf Strain (Medial Gastrocnemius — Tennis Leg)
description: Acute tear of medial head of gastrocnemius at musculotendinous junction.
key_findings: Sudden 'shot in the calf' sensation, posterior calf pain/swelling, antalgic gait.
diagnostic_tips: Ultrasound visualises tear and excludes DVT (Wells score).

treatment_plan.acute: POLICE, heel raise, crutches if antalgic, EXCLUDE DVT.
treatment_plan.subacute: Calf isometrics → isotonic, calf raise progression, gait normalisation.
treatment_plan.chronic: Plyometric progression, return-to-sport criteria; address calf stiffness.
